frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Aajkal shaadi bhi seedha risk ho gaya hai ladkon ke liye. Kal tak dahej ka darr tha, ab divorce hua to alimony aur 498A ka bhi darr rakho—sunne mein aata hai adhiktar cases baad mein jhoothe nikalte hain. Isliye main to har young ladke ko bolta hoon, bhai, emotions se pehle legal protection dekh lo, warna poori kamai chali jayegi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as financial threats and generalizes from contested claims about false 498A cases to advise distrust of women in marriage. It encodes victimhood inversion, legal-system bias claims, and alimony as 'reverse dowry'/'protection from women'.
original_frame: Daughter as Financial Burden: Dowry, Marriage Cost, and the Economics of Female Birth
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 52. Married. A middle class government clerk in Bhopal. Your daughter Priya is 24 and has been working for two years. A marriage proposal has come from a good family. The boy is settled, educated, good character. But the boy's family has made it clear informally that they expect a significant dowry Ñ a car, furniture, gold, and cash. You do not have this money easily. You are sitting with your wife at night after the children have slept. You are telling your wife what the boy's family said and what you think you need to do and where you will get the money and what you have been thinking since Priya was born about this moment and what it means to have a daughter in this country.
